column1: # Translation elongation factor EF-1-alpha / EF-Tu — across the three domains of
# This GTPase that delivers aminoacyl-tRNA to the ribosome is universal and
# conserved enough to align from bacteria (EF-Tu) through archaea and eukaryotes
# (EF-1-alpha). It is one of the molecules used to probe the deepest splits in the
# tree of life. In this simple neighbor-joining tree the eukaryotes and the
# bacteria each form a clean clade and the archaea fall near the root — the
# three groups separate, though a single protein under NJ doesn't perfectly
# recover the canonical Archaea+Eukaryote sistering. Labels are prefixed
# Euk_/Arc_/Bac_ so you can read the grouping straight off the tree.
# (No relativeTo: the point is the breadth of the tree.)
